action.reasoning: Observation: evaluation is failing purely because build_model_from_checkpoint is still selecting/constructing the wrong architecture for legacy checkpoints like ema_weighted.pt; command output shows all current checkpoints fail to load due to exact state_dict mismatches. Analysis: with only 7 iterations left and a strong existing public score from ema_weighted.pt, the highest-value move is not retraining but making model loading fully backward-compatible. The key is to infer variant directly from state_dict keys: legacy checkpoints have head.* and optional horizon_mixer/horizon params; end-conv checkpoints have end_conv_* and optional post.* / horizon params. Also forward must consistently accept [B,T,N] and convert to [B,1,N,T]. Decision: rewrite model.py cleanly with exact legacy and end-conv variants plus robust checkpoint extraction/inference, then run local smoke tests on all saved checkpoints and predict.py. If ema_weighted.pt loads and local predict succeeds, submit it immediately.
